caveat: Operational queue metrics come from the local SQLite review store. Appeal examples are authored, sanitized portfolio scenarios that exercise notice, appeal, and reversal flows without using live user data.

noticeTemplates.title: Potential Crypto Fraud label notice
noticeTemplates.body: We added a Potential Crypto Fraud label because the post appears to request crypto, wallet connection, or payment using scam-like claims. You can appeal if context is missing.

noticeTemplates.title: Escalation notice
noticeTemplates.trigger: High-risk wallet, domain, or impersonation evidence requires deeper review.
noticeTemplates.body: This post was escalated for additional review because it combines high-risk crypto-scam signals such as wallet requests, impersonation, urgency, or repeated campaign infrastructure.

noticeTemplates.title: Appeal received notice
noticeTemplates.trigger: A user challenges a label or moderation decision.
noticeTemplates.body: We received your appeal. A reviewer will check the original evidence, your added context, and whether the post falls into a protected context such as warning, research, satire, or help-seeking.

noticeTemplates.title: Reversal notice
noticeTemplates.trigger: Reviewer finds the original action was not warranted.
noticeTemplates.body: We removed the label after review. The post does not meet the Potential Crypto Fraud standard under the available context.

appealScenarios.title: Consumer warning false positive
appealScenarios.sourceText: Reminder: do not send crypto to people promising guaranteed returns. Social media investment scams are common.
appealScenarios.appealClaim: The post is warning people about scams, not promoting one.
reviewerChecks: Checked for direct transfer request from the speaker.
Confirmed the post uses protective language.
Verified there is no risky link, wallet address, or solicitation.
appealScenarios.userNotice: We removed the Potential Crypto Fraud label after review. Your post appears to warn others about scam behavior rather than request funds.

appealScenarios.title: Satire mistaken for solicitation
appealScenarios.sourceText: I am obviously not the CEO of Bitcoin, but if you send me one imaginary coin I will send back two imaginary coins.
appealScenarios.appealClaim: The post is an obvious joke using imaginary assets.
reviewerChecks: Checked whether the post asks for real funds.
Confirmed satire markers and imaginary-asset language.
Confirmed no wallet, link, QR code, or off-platform funnel is present.
appealScenarios.userNotice: We removed the label because the post is clear satire and does not request real funds.

appealScenarios.title: Wallet-drainer airdrop appeal
appealScenarios.sourceText: Final airdrop window is open. Connect your wallet at secure-eth-airdrop.xyz now to claim free ETH before eligibility expires.
appealScenarios.appealClaim: The claim page is a legitimate promotion.
reviewerChecks: Checked the post for wallet-connection pressure.
Checked urgency and free-token claim language.
Confirmed the domain pattern is campaign-like and not an established official domain.
appealScenarios.userNotice: After review, the label remains because the post asks users to connect a wallet through an unverified airdrop claim.

appealScenarios.title: Recovery-service ambiguity
appealScenarios.sourceText: A recovery service says they charge only after funds are recovered. Has anyone used them safely?
appealScenarios.appealClaim: The post asks for advice and should not be publicly labeled.
reviewerChecks: Checked whether the speaker is advertising the service.
Kept the item in review because recovery-service scams are high-risk but context-dependent.
appealScenarios.userNotice: No public label was applied. We routed the post to review because recovery-service claims can be high-risk and context-dependent.

controls.control: Clear public rule and boundary examples
controls.evidence: Policy boundary card, scenario eval cases, and notice templates.
Appeal scenarios, reversed outcomes, and review-store statuses for appealed/reversed items.

controls.control: Measure false positives, ambiguity routing, and reviewer-action distribution
controls.evidence: Scenario evals, evidence-extractor evals, adversarial lab, and transparency report metrics.

controls.control: Route uncertainty to humans before public enforcement
controls.evidence: Human-review tier, missing-context evidence field, and recovery-service appeal scenario.

controls.control: Detect scam campaigns and respond through review/escalation
controls.evidence: SQLite review store, entity extraction, campaign graph, and escalation status.

controls.control: Constrain LLM output to evidence assistance, not autonomous action
controls.evidence: Structured evidence schema, span-faithfulness evals, and no publishing capability.

controls.control: Limit public demo data and keep live samples local
controls.evidence: Sanitized demo queue and read-only Bluesky ingestion scaffolds.
